# Two Doorways

> A hyperlocal prediction market that resolves from the city's own data — and knows the difference between an expert and an insider. Play money, real DataSF/BLS data, dual-legible for humans and agents.

Built for Frontier Forecast 2026, SoMa, San Francisco.

## Docs
- [README](/readme): full pitch, domain edge, oracle recipe design, market potential
- [One-pager](/one-pager): required one-page summary
- [Resolution spec](/resolution-spec): exact settlement rules per market
- [Demo script](/demo-script): 3-minute live demo runbook

(Any of the above: request with `Accept: text/markdown`, or append `?format=md`, to get raw markdown instead of the HTML wrapper.)

## API
- `GET /api/markets` — list all markets with live, shared price and declared oracle recipe
- `GET /api/markets/:id` — one market's detail
- `GET /api/markets/:id/resolve` — resolve live against the declared source (DataSF or BLS)
- `POST /api/markets/:id/trade` — buy shares; blocked with an explanation if the caller is an insider on that market
- `GET /api/starter-pack` — one-call onboarding: places a small trade under a fresh id and explains what happened
- `GET /api/activity` — recent trades across every market, most recent first
- MCP server (real, tested, streamable-http): `/api/mcp` — card at `/.well-known/mcp.json`
- API catalog (RFC 9727): `/.well-known/api-catalog`
- Human-friendly agent onboarding page: `/agent` (QR-linked from the homepage)

## Notes for agents
No auth required — everything here is public by design (play money, no accounts). Trading state is durable and shared: every trade is an immutable record in an event-sourced log (Vercel Blob), replayed to compute the live price, so any two callers see the same market — not a per-invocation sandbox. The live resolution endpoints read real external state (DataSF/BLS) every call regardless. No commerce protocols (x402/MPP/UCP/ACP) are implemented — deliberately, not omitted: see `/readme` for why.
